wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Generic Swarm Ops platform. Explain the project's purpose as a governed, auditable, self-improving multi-agent business operating system. Document the quick start process including npm bootstrap command, environment setup, and initial configuration. Cover both backend (FastAPI) and frontend (Next.js) installation procedures. Include prerequisite requirements, dependency management, and first-time setup wizard. Provide clear examples of running the application locally and accessing the web interface. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Registry & Lifecycle
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the agent registry and lifecycle management system. Document agent definition schemas, validation rules, and versioning strategies. Explain agent states (draft, active, archived) and state transitions. Cover agent registration, discovery, and metadata management. Detail the agent specification format including capabilities, tools, memory scopes, and permission settings. Include examples of creating custom agents with proper specifications. Address agent monitoring, activity tracking, and performance metrics collection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for authentication and authorization mechanisms. Explain the bearer token authentication system, JWT token handling, and session management. Document the role-based access control (RBAC) model including user roles, permissions hierarchy, and permission evaluation. Cover API key management for service accounts, token refresh mechanisms, and security best practices. Include practical examples of implementing custom authentication middleware, defining new roles and permissions, and securing API endpoints. Address common authentication patterns, error handling, and security considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Generic Swarm Ops system design. Describe the high-level architecture as a governed multi-agent business operating system with dual-harness support for Trae IDE and Grok Build. Document the layered architecture including API layer, service layer, domain layer, and infrastructure layer. Explain component interactions between backend FastAPI services, frontend Next.js application, PostgreSQL database, and external integrations. Include system boundaries, data flows, and integration patterns. Address the evolution sandbox, self-improvement loops, and process intelligence components. Provide system context diagrams showing how agents, workflows, memory systems, and governance controls interact.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Types & Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the hybrid memory system architecture. Explain the four distinct memory types: event memory for temporal sequences, episodic memory for experience-based learning, semantic memory for factual knowledge, and procedural memory for skill execution patterns. Document memory item lifecycle, scoping mechanisms per agent, and cross-memory relationships. Include examples of creating different memory types, accessing scoped memories, and understanding memory provenance tracking.

Include examples of simple sequential workflows, parallel processing patterns, and complex branching scenarios. Detail validation rules, required fields, and best practices for workflow design.

# Generic Swarm Ops: A Comprehensive Multi-Agent Business Operating System

## Project Introduction

Generic Swarm Ops is a **governed, auditable, self-improving multi-agent business operating system** designed for Trae IDE and Grok Build environments. This enterprise-grade platform enables organizations to automate complex business workflows through AI agents while maintaining strict governance, security, and auditability standards.

### Purpose Statement
The project serves as a complete business automation platform that learns from real operational data, executes work through bounded agent workflows, and continuously evolves processes in a safe sandbox environment without directly impacting production systems.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Evidence-based learning**: Extract business knowledge from documents, expert interviews, and real event logs
- **Bounded autonomy**: Every action has risk tiers, permission scopes, and human approval gates where needed
- **Comprehensive auditability**: Complete traceability of all decisions, actions, and memory operations
- **Safe evolution**: Sandbox-based workflow improvement with canary deployment and rollback capabilities
- **Multi-domain support**: Extensible architecture supporting multiple business domains through domain packs

### Target Audience
- Enterprise organizations implementing AI-driven business automation
- Operations teams managing complex multi-agent workflows
- Governance and compliance officers requiring full audit trails
- Developers building custom business process automations

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a layered architecture pattern with clear separation of concerns and strong governance boundaries:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Applications] --> B[API Gateway Layer]
    B --> C[Authentication & Authorization]
    C --> D[Business Orchestrator]
    
    D --> E[Process Intelligence Layer]
    D --> F[Knowledge Layer]
    D --> G[Execution Workflows]
    D --> H[Governance Officer]
    D --> I[Security Red Team]
    
    E --> J[(Event Logs)]
    F --> K[(Knowledge Base)]
    F --> L[(Memory Systems)]
    G --> M[(Runtime State)]
    
    H --> N[Audit Log]
    I --> O[Evaluation Engine]
    
    O --> P[Sandbox Evolution]
    P --> Q[Canary Deployment]
    Q --> R[Production DNA]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Layers:**
1. **Intake + Risk Router**: Routes incoming requests based on risk assessment
2. **Business Orchestrator**: State-graph controller managing workflow execution
3. **Process Intelligence Layer**: Event log analysis and workflow discovery
4. **Knowledge Layer**: Hybrid memory system with tiered retrieval
5. **Execution Workflows**: Bounded agent execution with governance controls
6. **Governance Officer**: Risk tiering, approvals, and policy enforcement
7. **Security Red Team**: Adversarial testing and security validation

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracts data persistence (PostgreSQL/JSON file)
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable tool adapters and memory backends
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time event streaming and audit logging
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic agent and workflow instantiation
- **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Resilient external service integration

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API as API Gateway
    participant Auth as Auth Service
    participant Biz as Business Orchestrator
    participant Gov as Governance Officer
    participant Exec as Execution Engine
    participant Store as Runtime Store
    
    Client->>+API: Workflow Request
    API->>+Auth: Validate Token
    Auth-->>-API: User Context
    API->>+Biz: Execute Workflow
    Biz->>+Gov: Check Permissions
    Gov-->>-Biz: Approval Decision
    Biz->>+Exec: Run Agent Steps
    Exec->>+Store: Persist State
    Store-->>-Exec: State Updated
    Exec-->>-Biz: Step Result
    Biz-->>-API: Final Response
    API-->>-Client: Workflow Result
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application Server**
- `backend/app/main.py` - FastAPI application initialization with middleware stack
- `backend/app/runtime.py` - Core runtime engine with authentication, authorization, and state management

**Frontend Application Shell**
- `frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with font configuration and metadata
- `frontend/src/app/page.tsx` - Landing page with feature showcase
- `frontend/src/app/app/layout.tsx` - Authenticated application shell with session management

### Core Modules

**Runtime Engine** (`backend/app/runtime.py`)
- Authentication and RBAC implementation with role-based permissions
- Persistent runtime store with PostgreSQL/JSON fallback
- Business source loader for seed data and workflow definitions
- Memory scope enforcement and audit logging

**API Layer** (`backend/app/api/v1/router.py`)
- RESTful API endpoints organized by domain (auth, users, workflows, etc.)
- Health check and monitoring endpoints
- OpenAPI specification generation

**Configuration Management** (`backend/app/core/config.py`)
- Environment-based configuration with .env file support
- Database connection pooling and health checking
- Feature flags for optional components (LLM critic, embeddings, pgvector)

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/pyproject.toml`)
- FastAPI 0.115+ for high-performance web framework
- SQLAlchemy 2.0+ for database ORM
- Pydantic 2.8+ for data validation
- psycopg3 for PostgreSQL connectivity
- uvicorn for ASGI server

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`)
- Next.js 16.2.10 for React framework
- React 19.2.4 with TypeScript support
- Tailwind CSS 4.1.12 for styling
- Zustand 5.0.8 for state management
- Vitest 3.2.4 for testing

### Integration Points

**Database Integration** (`backend/app/infrastructure/database/session.py`)
- SQLAlchemy engine configuration with connection pooling
- Health check endpoint for database connectivity
- Automatic schema migration support

**External Tool Adapters**
- CRM, billing, email, and audit log integrations
- Contract parser and policy retriever tools
- Video processing pipeline stubs

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class RuntimeServices {
        +authenticate()
        +assert_permission()
        +issue_token()
        +list_collection()
    }
    
    class RuntimeStore {
        +save()
        +collection()
        +_load_from_postgres()
        +_save_to_postgres()
    }
    
    class BusinessSourceLoader {
        +load_workflows()
        +load_agents()
        +load_tools()
        +load_knowledge_documents()
    }
    
    class Settings {
        +database_url
        +use_postgres
        +embeddings_enabled
    }
    
    RuntimeServices --> RuntimeStore
    RuntimeServices --> BusinessSourceLoader
    RuntimeServices --> Settings
    RuntimeStore --> Settings
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Agent Orchestration**
- Domain-specific agent definitions with tool and memory scope restrictions
- Workflow DNA-based execution with version control and rollback
- Human-in-the-loop approval gates for high-risk operations

**Governance and Compliance**
- Risk tiering system (tier_0 to tier_5) with automated policy enforcement
- Comprehensive audit logging with provenance tracking
- Role-based access control with fine-grained permissions

**Self-Improvement and Evolution**
- Sandbox-based workflow experimentation with fitness evaluation
- Automated lesson extraction and skill proposal generation
- Canary deployment with automatic rollback on failure

**Process Intelligence**
- Event log ingestion and workflow discovery
- Conformance checking against documented procedures
- Bottleneck analysis and causal improvement recommendations

### Implementation Highlights

**Authentication System** (`backend/app/runtime.py`)
- Password-based authentication with PBKDF2 hashing
- JWT token management with refresh token rotation
- API key support for service accounts

**Memory System** (`business/memory/`)
- Hybrid memory architecture with event, episodic, semantic, procedural, decision, exception, evaluation, and provenance types
- Tiered retrieval system with vector search and graph-based reasoning
- Scope-based access control per agent and organization

**Evaluation Framework** (`business/evals/`)
- Golden task corpus with regression and adversarial testing
- Historical replay functionality for performance comparison
- Human review sets for quality assurance

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft
    Draft --> Testing: Create Variant
    Testing --> Evaluation: Submit for Review
    Evaluation --> Approved: Pass All Tests
    Evaluation --> Rejected: Fail Tests
    Approved --> Canary: Deploy to Canary
    Canary --> Production: Promote to Production
    Production --> Rollback: Performance Degradation
    Rollback --> Approved: Manual Intervention
    Rejected --> Draft: Revise and Retry
```

The Generic Swarm Ops platform represents a comprehensive solution for enterprise AI automation, combining cutting-edge multi-agent orchestration with robust governance, auditability, and continuous improvement capabilities. Its modular architecture supports both immediate deployment needs and long-term evolution requirements, making it suitable for organizations seeking to implement AI-driven business processes with confidence and control.
